parts.text:
Web scraping Amazon is technically challenging because the site uses heavy bot-detection measures. To successfully retrieve data using a script, you must include a valid `User-Agent` header to mimic a real browser; otherwise, the request will be blocked or redirected to a CAPTCHA page.

The following script uses `requests` for the HTTP call and `BeautifulSoup` for parsing.

### Prerequisites
`pip install requests beautifulsoup4 pandas`

### Python Script

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd

def scrape_amazon(search_query):
    url = f"https://www.amazon.com/s?k={search_query.replace(' ', '+')}"
    
    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/119.0.0.0 Safari/537.36",
        "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9"
    }

    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    if response.status_code != 200:
        print(f"Failed to retrieve page: Status {response.status_code}")
        return

    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, "html.parser")
    products = []

    for item in soup.select(".s-result-item[data-component-type='s-search-result']"):
        name = item.select_one("h2 a span")
        price_whole = item.select_one(".a-price-whole")
        price_fraction = item.select_one(".a-price-fraction")
        rating = item.select_one("i.a-icon-star-small span")

        if name:
            products.append({
                "Name": name.get_text(strip=True),
                "Price": f"{price_whole.get_text(strip=True)}.{price_fraction.get_text(strip=True)}" if price_whole else "N/A",
                "Rating": rating.get_text(strip=True).split(" ")[0] if rating else "N/A"
            })

    df = pd.DataFrame(products)
    df.to_csv("amazon_products.csv", index=False)
    print(f"Saved {len(products)} products to amazon_products.csv")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    scrape_amazon("laptop")
```

### Practical Note:
Amazon frequently changes its CSS selectors and utilizes IP-based rate limiting. If this script returns zero results, it is likely because the `User-Agent` was flagged or the HTML structure has updated. For large-scale tasks, developers typically use headless browsers (Playwright/Selenium) or rotating proxy services to bypass these protections.
